decisions.title: Create Team Button Color Change
decisions.summary: Modified the button color from indigo-purple to teal-purple gradient.

decisions.title: Angular 21 (Standalone Components)
decisions.summary: Using Angular 21 with standalone APIs instead of NgModules.

decisions.title: SSR + Hydration
decisions.summary: Server-side rendering and client hydration enabled with prerendering.

decisions.title: HTTP Client + REST API
decisions.summary: Angular HttpClient for backend communication with REST endpoints.

decisions.title: Environment-based Configuration
decisions.summary: API base URL controlled via Angular environments, with a potential inconsistency in some services.

decisions.title: Auth Service File
decisions.summary: Highly sensitive file requiring careful review for any modifications.

decisions.title: JWT Token in localStorage
decisions.summary: Storing access token in localStorage for authentication.

decisions.title: HTTP Interceptor for Bearer Token
decisions.summary: Using functional interceptor to add Bearer token to HTTP requests.

decisions.title: Route Protection
decisions.summary: Using functional guard to protect routes requiring authentication.

decisions.title: Angular Signals for Local State
decisions.summary: Using Angular signals for component/service state and computed for derived views.

decisions.title: RxJS Observables for IO
decisions.summary: Using RxJS Observables for HTTP calls and side-effects.

decisions.title: Angular Material + CDK
decisions.summary: Using Angular Material and CDK for UI components.

decisions.title: SweetAlert2 for Dialogs/Notifications
decisions.summary: Using SweetAlert2 for dialogs and notifications.

decisions.title: Chart.js via ng2-charts
decisions.summary: Using Chart.js with ng2-charts for charts.

decisions.title: Vitest + JSDOM for Testing
decisions.summary: Using Vitest and JSDOM for testing.

decisions.title: Global Styles + SCSS Theme
decisions.summary: Using global CSS and SCSS theme for styling.

decisions.title: Frontend API Target on Render
decisions.summary: Frontend API hosted on Render.

decisions.title: Add Task Editing Capability
decisions.summary: Implementing task editing to enhance user experience and reduce friction.

decisions.title: Architecture Pattern
decisions.summary: The application follows a feature-based structure with separation into Core, Features, and Shared layers.

decisions.title: Application Bootstrap
decisions.summary: Client entrypoint and root component configuration, along with provider setup.

decisions.title: SSR and Server Entrypoints
decisions.summary: Configuration for server-side rendering and server providers.

decisions.title: Routing Architecture
decisions.summary: Angular Router setup with route structure and protection mechanisms.

decisions.title: State Management
decisions.summary: Using Angular Signals for reactive state, including signals for user and task data.

decisions.title: HTTP Communication
decisions.summary: API integration details, base URL, authentication headers, and service structure.

decisions.title: Authentication Flow
decisions.summary: Step-by-step process for user authentication and token management.

decisions.title: Component Architecture
decisions.summary: Discussion on component patterns, communication, and key components like TaskBoard, Header, and CommentsComponent.

decisions.title: UI/UX Features
decisions.summary: UI toolkit, user feedback mechanisms, animations, and data visualization tools.

decisions.title: Data Models
decisions.summary: Core TypeScript interfaces for User, Teams, Projects, Tasks, and Comments.

decisions.title: Testing Strategy
decisions.summary: Test tooling and unit test structure.

decisions.title: Build and Deployment
decisions.summary: Development, production build, and server-side rendering instructions.

decisions.title: Design Patterns
decisions.summary: Observed design patterns like Dependency Injection, Observable, Guard, Interceptor, and Signal patterns.

decisions.title: Security Considerations
decisions.summary: Client-side security measures including JWT authentication, route guards, HTTP interceptor, and logout mechanism.

decisions.title: Performance and UX Optimizations
decisions.summary: Techniques to enhance performance and user experience.

decisions.title: Scalability Considerations
decisions.summary: Discussion on the application's scalability aspects.

decisions.title: Add ThemeService
decisions.summary: Implement ThemeService with persistence, apply theme, and provide necessary methods.

decisions.title: Initialize Theme Early
decisions.summary: Ensure theme initialization during app startup.

decisions.title: Add UI Toggle in Header
decisions.summary: Implement a toggle button in the header for theme switching.

decisions.title: Global Theming Styles
decisions.summary: Update CSS and SCSS files to support dark mode.

decisions.title: Log Sensitive Areas
decisions.summary: Update logs with any sensitive areas found during editing.

decisions.title: Run Checks
decisions.summary: Execute build and other checks to ensure functionality.

decisions.title: שינוי צבע כפתור "צור צוות"
decisions.summary: עדכון צבע הכפתור מ-טורקיז/סגול לכחול-סגלגל

decisions.title: Disable Task Editing
decisions.summary: Remove the ability to edit tasks via the ✏️ button and popup.

decisions.title: Add User-Facing Dark Mode Toggle
decisions.summary: Implement a visible toggle for users to switch between light and dark modes.

decisions.title: Persist Theme Preference
decisions.summary: Ensure the selected theme is saved and applied across refreshes.

decisions.title: Minimal Invasive CSS Changes
decisions.summary: Apply the theme with minimal changes to the existing CSS structure.

decisions.title: Avoid Breaking Angular Material Theming
decisions.summary: Ensure the dark mode implementation does not interfere with Angular Material theming.

decisions.title: Avoid Flash on Initial Load
decisions.summary: Initialize the theme early to reduce any visual flash during SSR rendering.

decisions.title: Add UI Toggle Button
decisions.summary: Implement a toggle button in the header for users to switch themes.

decisions.title: Define Global Theme Variables
decisions.summary: Set up CSS variables for light and dark mode themes and apply them globally.

decisions.title: Record Sensitive Areas
decisions.summary: Document any issues or sensitive areas encountered during implementation.

decisions.title: Manual Verification
decisions.summary: Manually check key screens for contrast and readability after implementation.

decisions.title: Refactor Header Colors
decisions.summary: Convert key header colors to CSS variables and add a toggle button.

decisions.title: Theme Application Mechanism
decisions.summary: Apply theme by setting data-theme on the <html> element, relying on browser execution.

decisions.title: Material Color-Scheme Switching
decisions.summary: Adjust color-scheme for Material components, but may require a full dark theme later.

decisions.title: Global Body Colors
decisions.summary: Set body background and text colors using CSS variables, with light/dark token sets.

rules.rule: The 'Create Team' button will now display a teal-to-purple gradient.
Maintain existing hover effects and transitions for the button.
Use a cooler teal tone to improve visual distinction.
Any modifications to the Auth Service file must be carefully reviewed and justified.
Changes to auth logic, token handling, or session management require extreme caution.
Use functional providers instead of module-based providers.
Use functional routing instead of module-based routing.
Avoid potential duplication by ensuring `provideRouter(routes)` appears only once.
Inject services instead of components to avoid tighter coupling and potential DI issues.
Allow full task updates, including title, description, and due date.
Display edit buttons (✏️ עריכה) on all task columns.
Open a modal dialog with editable fields for task details.
Validate that the task title is not empty before updating.
Send update requests to the backend API.
Update local task list and display success/error notifications.
Display a form with fields for title, description, priority, and due date.
Make the title field mandatory.
Show a success toast notification after a successful edit.
Handle errors, including permission checks, and display appropriate messages.
Automatically update the UI after a successful edit.
Use Angular 21 for the project.
Organize the application around auth, teams, projects, tasks, and comments domains.
Utilize standalone components instead of NgModules.
Use Angular Signals for local reactive state management.
Integrate RxJS and HttpClient for I/O operations.
Implement SSR (Server-Side Rendering) and hydration for improved performance.
Follow a feature-based architecture with Core, Features, and Shared layers.
Place shared services, guards, and interceptors in the Core layer.
Feature modules with their own components should reside in the Features layer.
Reusable components, directives, and models should be placed in the Shared layer.
Use Angular Router for routing with a defined route structure.
Protect routes using the AuthGuard.
Prerender routes on the server side.
Store JWT token in localStorage for authentication.
Validate session on startup via a GET request to /auth/me.
Attach the token to requests via an HTTP interceptor.
Use Angular Signals for reactive state, including signals for current user and task list.
Define computed signals for filtered task lists by status.
Implement a computed signal for tasks filtered by the current user.
Use Angular Material and CDK for the UI toolkit.
Utilize SweetAlert2 for modal dialogs and user feedback.
Implement loading states using signals for async operations.
Integrate ng2-charts for data visualization.
Define core TypeScript interfaces for data models.
Use Vitest and JSDOM for testing.
Develop unit tests for services and components.
Use npm start for development and ng serve on localhost:4200.
Build for production using npm run build.
Implement server-side rendering (SSR) using Angular SSR and Express.
Utilize hydration with event replay for better UX.
Apply minification, optimization, and hashing for production builds.
The feature-based structure allows for easy addition of new features.
Centralize business logic in the service layer.
Use shared models for type safety across the application.
Configure environment settings for easy deployment to different environments.
The modular architecture enables extraction of components to libraries.
Persist theme in localStorage with default light mode.
Apply theme via document.documentElement.dataset.theme.
Provide init(), toggle(), setTheme(), and isDark() methods in ThemeService.
Initialize themeService.init() in src/app/app.ts.
Update header.html, header.ts, and header.css to add a toggle button and hook to ThemeService.
Define base CSS variables and dark overrides in styles.css.
Switch color-scheme based on data-theme='dark' in material-theme.scss.
Run npm run build and other configured checks.

Prevent users from changing historical task data.
Remove the edit button and associated popup flow from the UI.
Add a client-side guard to block edits to title, description, and due date.
Ensure users can still create, delete, change status/priority, and assign tasks.
Use CSS Variables for Theme Control
Align with Angular Material Theming
Store Theme Preference in Local Storage
Initialize Theme Early for SSR
Add Toggle Button in Header
Update Global CSS with Theme Variables
Document Implementation Issues
Run Tests and Linting
Verify Contrast and Readability
Avoid large refactor, introduce CSS variables gradually.
Prevent theme flash on load, apply theme early during bootstrap.
Logo gradient remains hard-coded for now.
ThemeService guards localStorage access, but document access assumes client-side execution.
Material components may require a full dark theme integration.
Feature-level component styles may need incremental variable refactors.
